upstream_note: v2: full 6x6. Top-level cells are the openai-egress row for v1 compatibility; the full grid is under upstreams.<egress>.cells keyed by ingress dialect.
capability_note: advisory context only (probe-first: every cell is probed regardless): Bifrost v1.6.4 has no openai-chat -> responses bridge and no gemini/cohere/bedrock-format ingress; undeclared cells are grey by that capability limit

openai.verdict_note: HTTP 200, passthrough (same dialect, no translation required); round trip to the mock's openai endpoint confirmed

perf.reverify_note: post-load re-verify probe could not re-confirm (miss, a transient dead socket, not a misroute); the number stands on the capability probe plus the successful load sweep

openai-responses.verdict_note: HTTP 502 on POST /v1/responses, persistent across 2 attempts with the mock verifiably healthy: a deterministic application-level rejection of this ingress/egress pairing, not a transport failure
openai-responses.body_snippet: {"is_bifrost_error":false,"status_code":502,"error":{"error":"the server closed connection before returning the first response byte. Make sure the server returns 'Connection: close' response header be
openai-responses.probe_note: probe failed: HTTP 502 on POST /v1/responses (persistent across 2 attempts, mock healthy); first bytes: {"is_bifrost_error":false,"status_code":502,"error":{"error":"the server closed connection before returning the first response byte. Make sure the server return

anthropic.verdict_note: HTTP 502 on POST /anthropic/v1/messages, persistent across 2 attempts with the mock verifiably healthy: a deterministic application-level rejection of this ingress/egress pairing, not a transport failure
anthropic.body_snippet: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"provider_connection_failed","message":"failed to execute HTTP request to provider API"}}
anthropic.probe_note: probe failed: HTTP 502 on POST /anthropic/v1/messages (persistent across 2 attempts, mock healthy); first bytes: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"provider_connection_failed","message":"failed to execute HTTP request to provider API"}}

openai.verdict_note: untestable: no upstream base-URL override reaches the test mock (a mock-reachability limit of this rig, not gateway incapability). Bifrost v1.6.4 hardcodes the Bedrock host (bedrock-runtime.<region>.amazonaws.com, core/providers/bedrock/bedrock.go; network_config.base_url never overrides it), so the harness mock cannot stand in for the upstream; Bifrost does serve Bedrock Converse in production

openai-responses.verdict_note: HTTP 200 with a valid openai-responses envelope, but the gateway did not speak the bedrock dialect to the upstream: the mock received the request on the cohere endpoint instead
openai-responses.probe_note: probe failed: HTTP 200; upstream request landed on the cohere endpoint, not the bedrock endpoint (this pairing is not wired to a bedrock upstream)

anthropic.verdict_note: HTTP 200 with a valid anthropic envelope, but the gateway did not speak the bedrock dialect to the upstream: the mock received the request on the cohere endpoint instead
anthropic.probe_note: probe failed: HTTP 200; upstream request landed on the cohere endpoint, not the bedrock endpoint (this pairing is not wired to a bedrock upstream)

hardware: AWS m7g.4xlarge (Graviton3, 16 cores / 64 GB). Gateway-under-test pinned to 4 cores (the comparable basis); mock and load generator on 6 cores each so the mock never bottlenecks the streaming sweep. Ubuntu 24.04. One dedicated box per gateway.
